dataList.flavor: An A+ class Workshop weapon that has remained a constant companion of its wielder, the Fixer at the Crack of Dawn, since his first days as a Fixer through the Smoke War and its fallout. Every contract successfully carried out by the Fixer added to Stigma Workshop's fame, and as a show of gratitude, they repeatedly upgraded and expanded the weapon's capabilities. Just as the Fixer's seasoned renown was built up over time, this blade, too, was forged, remade, and sharpened by the years. Morgenfeuer, once regarded as an S-class weapon, inevitably lost its fervor and edge in the twilight years of the Fixer at the Crack of Dawn as he withdrew from the front lines of the Fixer business in favor of teaching the next generation.

dataList.flavor: If you have made up your mind to use Workshop gears that utilize heat, then you had best be prepared to pay a slew of hidden costs. Unlike conventional weapons, whose variables are in the more obviously observable territory, such as sharpness and durability, weapons that radiate heat require all manner of groundwork to ensure that the wielder is not consumed by that very heat. And that requires money. It is, of course, a simple enough thing to procure weapons that set things ablaze outright or fabrics that do not catch fire easily. But to obtain a weapon whose heat output can be fine-tuned and controlled at any given moment according to the wielder's every need, or outlier fabrics that ward off heat completely—such exceptional gear costs not only a fortune but also the wielder's constant training to be as exceptional in handling them. Naturally, if you have invested that much money and time, the returns will come in the form of many more contracts requiring your services.

dataList.flavor:
The appeal of a weapon shaped like a suitcase or a musical instrument case is clear enough.

First, the user may blend into the cover of daily life without raising alarm. Though arms of this sort are unwieldy and complicated to make use of in battle, that perceived shortcoming makes them less likely to be recognized as tools of killing.

Secondly, weapons fashioned into the form of a case afford storage capacity. Container-shaped arms, for example, are actively used by the Fixers of the Devyat' Association.

Such convenience, however, does not automatically win favors from those who elect Workshop gear as their weapon of choice. The common opinion is that anyone who invests so much time in mastering this sort of weaponry would be better served by training with something else—in fact, the user would often hear snide remarks from other combatants, ranging from "Fixers these days" to "showy and pretentious."

But should one let this reputation for impracticality drop one's guard when facing an opponent armed with such a finicky piece of equipment, especially if they happen to be a high-ranking Fixer or a Syndicate officer, one will find that the laughable "containers" or "cases" carry quite a different weight—a weight grave enough for any Workshop worth its name to call them "coffins." And coffins, as a matter of fact, have a way of inviting butterflies.
